titleStmt.title: Letter from Susan Fitzgerald to Michael Gorman, 12 October 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Susan Fitzgerald

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Susan Fitzgerald (d. 1980) to Michael Gorman. The letter refers mainly to arranging one of their secret meetings as Michael will be back from Dublin. She writes that can only meet if the weather is fine. Susan also refers to a dance in Maryborough (now Portaoise) and a letter from a professor in Albert College.Michael Gorman was one of a large Catholic family living at a farm in Ballinalug, County Laois (then Queen's County). Susan Fitzgerald was one a Church of Ireland family living in Raheenahone House near Stradbally, County Laois, also in the Slieve Bloom mountains. At the time the letter was written they were involved in a courtship but owing to the religious difference the love affair had to be kept secret. Eventually both were in Dublin: he in the Albert Agricultural College in Glasnevin (where he eventually became Professor of Agricultural Botany and Biochemistry) and she as a volunteer nurse in the Royal Hospital in Baggot Street. They eventually went to London to marry.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Note at the top left-hand side of the page written sideways I wish you could come
by an earlier train -
I'll be at home at
4 p.m.
Molly sends xx one
for each cheek Raheena house Stradbally Thursday Dearest Mickie I was very
pleased to get your
letter on Tuesday and
Im so glad you
are able to come down.
Note listed on the side you need never tell me Im a bad cyclist again
the way you are behaving!
sake dont give him my address
in case he might think of
writing but indeed I know he wouldnt
be bothered & I hope so.
There was a great concert
in Maryboro' on Wednesday. I
was asked to go but I didnt
Im waiting for the first week
in January!! I had a long
letter from Katie McCallinan
& she wanted to know if I
was going up this month. I shall look forward with
the greatest possible pleasure
to Saturday & I hope it will
be a nice sun shiny day. With heaps of love for
always you own little girl Sue.
M.J. Gorman Esq, Albert College Glasnevin Dublin
